title: barista pour — structured output
description: Top-level document emitted by `barista pour --output json`. Reports the outcome of materializing locked artifacts into a target directory (default: `~/.m2/repository`). Under `--dry-run` no files are written and `materialized` is `0`; the `planned-paths` array still lists every destination that would have been created so callers can predict the filesystem effect.

command.description: Discriminator. Always the literal string `pour` for documents produced by `barista pour`.

target.description: Absolute path of the directory artifacts were (or would be) materialized into.

scope.description: Scope filter that was applied (e.g. `compile`, `runtime`, `test`). Entries with other scopes were skipped.

considered.description: Total number of entries in the lockfile, before scope filtering.

planned.description: Number of entries selected after scope filtering. Equal to the length of `planned-paths`.

materialized.description: Number of entries actually materialized into `target`. `0` under `--dry-run`. In real runs, equals `planned` on success.

dry-run.description: Whether this was a `--dry-run`. When `true`, no files were created and `materialized` is `0`.

planned-paths.description: Destination paths that were (or would be) written, in planning order. Length equals `planned`.
